sources.title: StatsBomb Open Data - FIFA World Cup 2022
sources.license: Public Data User Agreement permits analysis/research and shared conclusions but restricts raw-data reproduction/distribution and commercial use; written permission required for this prize-entry use
sources.schema_units: JSON events, lineups, and 360 freeze frames; StatsBomb coordinates; timestamps by period
sources.coverage: Discovery found 64 FIFA World Cup 2022 matches and a 360 file for every match
sources.known_gaps: 360 is event-linked freeze-frame context, not continuous tracking; visible-area polygons can omit players; counterfactual outcomes are not observed
sources.product_use: Research-only candidate for a future event-snapshot rehearsal; not cleared for the public competition build
sources.next_gate: Request written permission covering prize-entry use, derived scenario publication, public GitHub, and hosted product before any product integration

sources.title: Soccer Match Event Dataset - Events
sources.publisher: Luca Pappalardo and Emanuele Massucco via Figshare; events collected by Wyscout
sources.version_season: Events item v1, DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.7770599.v1; FIFA World Cup 2018 subset
sources.license: CC BY 4.0 as displayed on the Figshare Events item; attribution and change indication required
sources.sha256: events.zip 877e015b716ffdeea18f04418e3f24fed307ed03c37ff305cabe1f47c4822a45 (77323413 bytes); events_World_Cup.json d789b7cd80671a0dd1263150e997d1450e1ed22cddc8beb7bb2a6266b374a869 (29981214 bytes); Figshare API computed_md5 7c20e8647e7eda58d7838a0c7b1ec6ab
sources.schema_units: JSON spatio-temporal match events with event time, type/subtype, player/team, tags, and normalized positions
sources.coverage: Locally verified: 101759 events across 64 matches; 603 corners across 32 teams; all corners have two position objects; 412 carry the accurate tag
sources.known_gaps: No full-player tracking or continuous ball path; event positions cannot prove a defender would intercept or a goal would be prevented
sources.product_use: Derive attributed 10-second Brazil corner windows, endpoint/marker contact summaries, boundary-robust Skeptic examples, and a separate defending-team outlet-band evidence column for Corner War Room
sources.next_gate: Keep the Korean straight-line rendering disclaimer beside every browser receipt and rerun the bound transform test before both freezes

sources.title: Soccer Match Event Dataset - Matches
sources.publisher: Luca Pappalardo and Emanuele Massucco via Figshare; match data collected by Wyscout
sources.version_season: Matches item v1, DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.7770422.v1; FIFA World Cup 2018 subset
sources.license: CC BY 4.0 as displayed on the Figshare Matches item; attribution and change indication required
sources.sha256: matches.zip c8f92bb7533e5c127e043cee764c991b5c25b4f5e70a65be931baae0b1765ce9 (645097 bytes); matches_World_Cup.json 1ddab20c8605c063a62341eb846466c8d040885a5f0f9a3e26d023123786abb6 (395677 bytes); publisher MD5 51d80beb17480919f69a53a0152c2d71
sources.schema_units: JSON match metadata including competition ID, participating teams, date, duration, winner, venue, and formation/lineup metadata
sources.coverage: Locally verified: 64 FIFA World Cup 2018 match records mapping all 101759 inspected events
sources.known_gaps: Metadata does not provide continuous tracking or validate tactical counterfactuals; team/player display rights remain separate from data licensing
sources.product_use: Map World Cup event sequences to opponent, match, and minute evidence in Corner War Room
sources.next_gate: Keep match labels source-linked and avoid crests, flags, photos, formation truth, or counterfactual claims

sources.title: Enhanced Football Intelligence - Explanation Document
sources.publisher: FIFA High Performance - Football Performance Analysis and Insights
sources.license: Reference-only pending rights review; do not redistribute PDF or presentation graphics
sources.schema_units: Methodology and terminology document, not row-level data
sources.coverage: Defines possession control, phases of play, recovery time, line breaks, team shape, final-third entries, forced turnovers, pressure, and xG
sources.known_gaps: Does not provide the underlying FIFA tracking data and does not validate this project's derived counterfactual geometry
sources.product_use: Terminology reference only; prevent misuse of tactical terms and clearly separate FIFA definitions from project calculations
sources.next_gate: Cite only the necessary terminology in planning documentation and keep it out of the derived-data pipeline

sources.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Post Match Summary Report - Match 28 Mexico v Korea Republic
sources.license: Research reference only; published FIFA terms do not establish this project's public prize/GitHub/app/YouTube/transformation/derived-output bundle, so the project risk gate requires source-specific written permission
sources.local_path: not retained; temporary research copy removed after audit
sources.schema_units: PDF aggregate match report with formations, phases, team geometry, line-break methods, pressure directions, turnovers, recovery, and physical metrics
sources.coverage: One official 2026 match report inspected from the 52-report match-report hub
sources.known_gaps: Aggregates have no event-row IDs, timestamps, or numeric pressure coordinates; through/around/over are line-break methods, not pitch channels; no counterfactual scoring
sources.product_use: Research-only input for the 2026 Press Pact challenger; no metric, image, logo, flag, or graphic may enter the deployed app yet
sources.next_gate: Satisfy the project risk gate with source-specific written permission covering extraction, transformation, display, public GitHub, YouTube, prize-entry use, and attribution; then audit three reports and 30 cells

sources.title: OpenFootball World Cup 2026 source and generated JSON
sources.version_season: upstream worldcup commit 606b6747ed8e0026aaf25ef86cb09d603da9844f; generated worldcup.json commit 5e4bc62f9e711f3ea83d2b150ac3200e7e9c90a0; 2026 season
sources.license: CC0 1.0 Universal as declared by both repositories; license file SHA-256 36ffd9dc085d529a7e60e1276d73ae5a030b020313e6c5408593a6ae2af39673
sources.local_path: not retained; inspected in a temporary directory; future pinned raw file belongs at data/raw/openfootball/worldcup-2026.json
sources.schema_units: Generated JSON match records with round/number, date, time and UTC offset, team labels, optional score/goals, group, and ground; upstream Football.TXT source
sources.coverage: Pinned JSON parses to 104 canonical match tuples and 48 team labels; 102 score-present records and 2 future score-absent fixtures at retrieval; 72 group records omit match number and 32 knockout records include it; current tournament context only
sources.known_gaps: Manually updated pin-time snapshot, not live; no explicit completion/status field; team naming is provider text and differs across providers; ground is location text, not guaranteed stadium; no event, tracking, formation, pressure, line-height, or physical metrics; no crest/flag/photo rights
sources.product_use: Potentially show provenance-linked 2026 tournament/fixture context with score/goals stripped, separately from the 2018 historical tactical evidence; no cross-provider team join or tactical use
sources.next_gate: After P0 freeze and organizer scope clarification, implement a checksum-verified fixture-only transform with canonical keys, nullable match numbers, fixed-offset time parsing, RFC3339 snapshot metadata, and isolation tests; re-pin before both freezes

sources.title: D2 Coding Font 1.3.2
sources.license: SIL Open Font License 1.1; Reserved Font Names D2Coding and D2Coding-Bold; upstream explicitly permits embedding and redistribution with the license
sources.schema_units: TrueType regular and bold Korean-capable font programs plus the upstream OFL 1.1 text
sources.coverage: All Korean and Latin text embedded in the eight-page planning PDF; no app runtime use
sources.known_gaps: Monospaced display face is less editorial than a proportional sans; layout must be rerendered and visually checked after the font change
sources.product_use: Deterministically render and embed licensed Korean glyphs in the planning PDF without relying on a machine-local proprietary system font
sources.next_gate: Keep exact font and license hashes in the planning harness; promote the asset only after its tracked-file audit is separated from football-data acceptance semantics

sources.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Dataset
sources.publisher: Mominullptr; upstream values attributed only at domain level to FIFA, Sofascore, and FBref
sources.license: Repository claims CC0, but upstream provider rights and row-level provenance are not established
sources.schema_units: CSV match, team, player, event, and summary tables
sources.coverage: Discovery sample only; no stable release and multiple inspected possession pairs did not total 100
sources.known_gaps: Unresolved upstream rights, domain-only provenance, provider-definition differences, internal consistency failures, and no stable release
sources.product_use: None; do not use as evidence or as a fallback for FIFA report metrics
sources.next_gate: No action unless the publisher supplies row-level provenance, upstream reuse rights, a stable release, and corrected consistency tests

note: The two CC BY 4.0 Figshare World Cup 2018 sources are accepted for Corner War Room after pinned ZIP/extracted hashes, deterministic transformation, 42/42 structural and semantic review, corrected half-open sensitivity, source lineage, and attribution checks. Other sources retain their own pending/rejected states.
